zh.lifeSummary: 當John le Jewe於1373年出生在英國戴文時，他的父親William le Jewe23歲，母親Alice Pilleston52歲。他與Alice De Pilsdon在英國英格蘭德比夏爾德比結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1416年10月18日在英國英格蘭多瑟特皮爾達享年43歲，埋葬在英國多瑟特皮爾達。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John le Jewe于1373年出生在英国戴文时，他的父亲William le Jewe23岁，母亲Alice Pilleston52岁。他与Alice De Pilsdon在英国英格兰德比夏爾德比结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1416年10月18日在英国英格兰多瑟特皮爾達享年43岁，埋葬在英国多瑟特皮爾達。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Jewe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and Cornwall): variant of   Jewell  .

variants.name: Jewell
meanings: English (Devon and Cornwall): from an Old Breton personal name, Iudhael, composed of elements meaning ‘lord’ + ‘generous, bountiful’ (see Jewkes ), which was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, a king of Brittany who abdicated and spent the last part of his life in a monastery. Forms of this name are found in medieval records not only in Devon and Cornwall, where they are of native origin, but also in East Anglia and even Yorkshire, where they were imported by Bretons after the Norman Conquest. The name was seemingly reintroduced from France in the 17th century by Huguenots.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John le Jewe was born in 1373, in Devon, England, his father, William le Jewe, was 23 and his mother, Alice Pilleston, was 52. He married Alice De Pilsdon in Derby, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 18 October 1416, in Pilsdon, Dorset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Pilsdon, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John le Jewe est né en 1373, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, William le Jewe, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Alice Pilleston, avait 52 ans. Il a épousé Alice De Pilsdon à Derby, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 18 octobre 1416, à Pilsdon, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Pilsdon, Dorset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John le Jewe 1373 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William le Jewe 23 und seine Mutter Alice Pilleston 52. Er hat in Derby, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom Alice De Pilsdon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 18. Oktober 1416 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Pilsdon, Dorset, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Pilsdon, Dorset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John le Jewe è nato il nel 1373 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, William le Jewe, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Alice Pilleston, aveva 52 anni. Lui sposato Alice De Pilsdon nel Derby, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 18 ottobre 1416, nel Pilsdon, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Pilsdon, Dorset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John le Jeweが1373年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父William le Jeweは23歳，母Alice Pillestonは52歳であった。イギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアデルビーでAlice De Pilsdonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1416年10月18日にイギリスイギリスドールセトピルスドンで43歳で死去し，イギリスドールセトピルスドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John le Jewe(이)가 1373년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William le Jewe(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Pilleston(은)는 52세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 더비에서 Alice De Pilsdon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1416년 10월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 피즈던에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 피즈던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John le Jewe nasceu em 1373, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William le Jewe, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Alice Pilleston, tinha 52 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice De Pilsdon em Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 18 de outubro de 1416, em Pilsdon, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Pilsdon, Dorset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John le Jewe родился в 1373 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени William le Jewe было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Pilleston, 52. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice De Pilsdon в местности дирби, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 18 октября 1416 г. в местности пилсдон, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности пилсдон, дорсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John le Jewe nació en 1373, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William le Jewe, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Alice Pilleston, tenía 52 años. Se casó con Alice De Pilsdon en Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 18 de octubre de 1416, en Pilsdon, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Pilsdon, Dorset, Inglaterra.
